message:
wip(planner): wire PLANNER-3 order_join_inputs_with_hints into INNER-join fast path (gate-only stage)

Follow-up to 77039de9 (PLANNER-3 permutation primitive) and 319bb0e7
(sqlite_stat1 row-count readers). This lands the first half of the
PLANNER-3 integration into the fast-path multi-table FROM scan:

- While walking `from.joins`, track whether every join is an `Inner`
  kind. The existing gate already rejected NATURAL joins and anything
  other than Inner|Left; the new `all_inner_joins` flag records
  whether Left appeared so we know whether reordering is sound.
- When every join is inner and there are ≥ 2 tables, consult
  `sqlite_stat1_row_counts()` to build a `Vec<TableRefWithStats>` for
  each source, marking `has_stats = true` only when stat1 actually
  had a row for that table. `n_pages` is a best-effort estimate
  derived from `n_rows.max(1)`; precise page counts require pager
  introspection which will arrive with the reshape step.
- Call `fsqlite_planner::order_join_inputs_with_hints(&refs)` and
  detect whether the returned permutation is the identity.

This intentionally stops at the gate: if the permutation is non-identity,
we emit a `tracing::debug!` on target `fsqlite::planner` and fall
through to source-order execution. The reshape itself — permuting
`table_sources`, `table_rows`, `join_plans`, `col_map` /
`col_collations`, and `projection_indices` together — is deferred to
a follow-up commit because it requires:

  (a) remapping `equi_pairs` indices, which index into the concatenated
      row layout, into their new positions after permutation, and
  (b) recomputing positional `left_width` values in `join_plans` since
      those are indices relative to the permuted prefix, not the
      original source order, and
  (c) a join-graph connectivity check so we don't reorder across
      disconnected equi-join components (a permutation that satisfies
      each individual ON predicate locally but moves the second
      component ahead of a filter that only its first component
      binds is unsound).

Landing the gate alone is forward progress:

  * it proves the dependency wiring compiles and runs on the hot path
    without panicking or deadlocking;
  * it establishes the LEFT/RIGHT/FULL → skip-reorder invariant at the
    exact site the reshape will need it;
  * it surfaces non-identity permutations in observability (target
    `fsqlite::planner`) so we can audit in staging how often stat1-based
    reordering *would* fire before actually turning it on;
  * the correctness story is trivial: unused result → no behavioral
    change versus the prior commit.

The `let _ = permutation;` silences the unused-binding warning that
would otherwise appear once the reshape path lands — that line is
intentionally vestigial and will be replaced by the reshape call site.

A `TODO(PLANNER-3 reshape)` comment documents the remapping strategy
and connectivity check so the follow-up commit has a concrete contract
to satisfy.

No-op at runtime for all callers. No new public API.
